missed_changes: Newly emphatic bullishness on the US regulatory/political shift post-2024 election as a major tailwind ('the regulatory environment has changed completely,' '$500b GO button,' 'couldn't have happened under the Biden administration,' praise for Brian Quintenz, anti-debanking advocacy) — a catalyst the 'neutral' risk stance did not anticipate.

Developed and published a formal token taxonomy (network tokens vs company-backed tokens vs memecoins + 7 categories), elevating 'network tokens' as the canonical good category — a new intellectual framework extending, but more elaborate than, the prior token-first/memecoin concerns.
New concrete push on light clients (Helios) and rollup interoperability as 'fundamental to Ethereum scaling' — a specific new direction within the L2 thesis.

Sharper crypto×AI specifics: AI agents owning wallets, 'decentralized autonomous chatbots' via TEEs, AI models running autonomously onchain as 'investable persons,' and a possible first AI-owned blockchain.
Adoption thesis around converting passive holders into active users ('only 5–10% of crypto owners are active'; State of Crypto user-sizing of 30–60M real monthly users).

Mild softening toward multichain pragmatism / Bitcoin appreciation ('support the best entrepreneurs... wherever that is,' 'incredible that we have bitcoin,' classifying BTC and even DOGE as network tokens).
notes: Unusually well-grounded prediction: every stance was reaffirmed in the window, none contradicted. Strongest confirmations are ZK/zkVMs (extensive Jolt work: Twist+Shout, memory/speed roadmap, Rust ZK hiring) and proof-of-personhood/identity (Eddy's own Big Ideas entry + a long Dec-2024 podcast). Stablecoins (RAI/algorithmic-stablecoin tweet), token-first concern ('if the tokenomics is just a pie chart, there is no network'), and memecoins (sustained derogation and definitional work) are clearly his personal views. Fully-on-chain games and DeFi rest partly on the co-authored/co-presented State of Crypto report plus the continuing EVE Frontier collaboration and DEX/fee-switch fundamentals discussion — slightly thinner but stance-consistent. The chief un-anticipated developments are the regulatory-regime optimism and the elaborate network-token taxonomy.
